mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline or persistently low level of the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of low FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant excess returns versus periods of high FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an emerging labor-market deterioration that forecasts weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE rose >0.3pp in 3 months, forward 60-day SPX returns were positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 in walk-forward testing restricted to triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive co-movement between INDPRO growth and RSAFS growth indicates that supply-side output expansion is being matched by demand-side retail absorption, jointly signaling a healthy production-consumption cycle that predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone captures this synchronization effect.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-RSAFS co-movement signal fails to outperform either single-series baseline in out-of-sample Rank IC, or produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing recovery regime, an upward trend in HOUST (housing starts) signals improving consumer confidence and construction-driven demand, which leads equity markets higher over a quarterly horizon as investors price in broader economic momentum. This effect is weak or absent outside housing-led recovery periods.
failure_condition: During periods independently classified as housing recovery, HOUST uptrend fails to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 restricted to that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in month-over-month PAYEMS growth below its trailing 6-month average signals an abrupt weakening in hiring momentum that triggers short-horizon risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: monthly PAYEMS growth falls below trailing 6-month average growth rate
failure_condition: Triggered PAYEMS deceleration events are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A steady upward trend in CPIAUCSL (headline CPI) without a corresponding FEDFUNDS response erodes real corporate earnings expectations and raises anticipated future policy tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL trend and forward 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of rising CPI show no significant underperformance versus flat/falling CPI periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In an expansion regime defined by positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth, continued real GDP growth reinforces earnings growth expectations and supports risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; in contraction regimes the same signal loses predictive power or reverses.
failure_condition: Within periods classified as expansion, GDPC1 growth fails to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of quarters, or the direction of the effect flips sign inconsistently across expansion sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near a policy floor (below 0.5%) and CPIAUCSL simultaneously accelerates above its trailing 12-month trend, the combination signals building expectations of imminent policy tightening despite an already-easy stance, causing a short-horizon negative repricing of equities as markets anticipate rate hikes before official policy action. This interaction effect is not observable from either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds trailing 12-month average
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary policy with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: Triggered joint FEDFUNDS-low/CPIAUCSL-accelerating events are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series baseline by a meaningful margin in walk-forward tests.
